status: CLOSED — Φ gate r5 = FAIL (3/6 L2, 4/6 KL); p3_p4 L2 0.1753 > 0.1471 p95 (worse than r4 0.1427); p2_p4 also newly failing (L2 0.2230, KL 0.1520)
cascade_parent: state/convergence/h100_stage2_r4_20260424.json (r4 edge-FAIL @ p3_p4 L2=0.1427 → r14 full-corpus retrain)
purpose: Stage-2 r5 on full r14 corpus (1200 bilingual pairs, sha256 21fcfa51b92f129b119d7fa42303adf7916547ef71c80c16f08e53839bf52b0b) — test whether fuller Korean-balanced corpus closes the p3_p4 L2 edge (r4: 0.1427 > p95 0.131). PASS target: L2 6/6 + KL 6/6 + p3_p4 < 0.10.
policy_ssot: .roadmap POLICY R1-R6 (완성도 기준, loss-free edits, weakest-link evidence first)

attempt_1.result: ABORT — auto-kickoff chain died at step 1/4 ('waiting for SSH') when prior session Bash tool terminated; chain process was a subprocess of Bash call, not detached
attempt_1.post_mortem: 16 H100 idle for ~68 min, $63 burned with 0% training

attempt_2.result: ABORT — bootstrap rc=99 on all 4 pods. Two latent bugs surfaced:
attempt_2.root_cause_1: bash 3.2 cannot parse `declare -A PID_WAIT` (added post-r4 in commit 773dc2fa); macOS /bin/bash is 3.2 and post_launch_chain.bash shebang is /bin/bash. Chain syntax-errored before step 1.
attempt_2.root_cause_2: bootstrap BOOTSTRAP_URL extraction via `sed 's/.*=//'` was greedy — RunPod presigned URL has 5+ `=` (X-Amz-Credential/Date/Signature query params) so only the final signature-hash trailer remained → curl tried to resolve a 64-char hex as hostname → DNS fail → bootstrap 99

after_fixes: declare -A removed + replaced with plain `state_pN` per-pod vars (loss-free)
sed non-greedy: `sed 's/^[^=]*=//' | tr -d '\\'` (extracts only first `=` value, strips any literal backslashes from heredoc escapes)
attempt_3.result: CHAIN COMPLETE at 15:04:29Z — SSH OK (12s), bootstrap DONE 4/4 (2m3s), driver shipped 4/4 (12s), 4 trainings kicked off nohup bg
attempt_3.comment: third time's the charm — root causes isolated + fixed, execution deterministic

incidents.symptom: chain script died silently at SSH-wait step after prior Bash session ended
incidents.root_cause: nohup ignores SIGHUP but not SIGTERM; the Bash tool's subprocess was killed when its shell terminated, taking the auto-kickoff chain with it
incidents.fix: relaunch via `(cmd >log 2>&1 & disown) &` detach pattern; later also tried `screen -dmS` when declare -A bug first surfaced
incidents.prevention: future chain launches should use `setsid` (Linux) or homebrew bash4 `screen -dmS` (macOS) for true session detachment — not just `nohup &` under parent shell

incidents.symptom: bootstrap rc=99 all 4 pods; ssh error = stderr `DNS resolve failed for c2d79eb20cfd...`
incidents.root_cause: post_launch_chain.bash line 78 uses `sed 's/.*=//'` to extract value from `echo URL=...` and `echo SHA=...` lines. RunPod fetch_hexa returns presigned S3 URLs with 5+ query-param `=` signs; greedy match kept only the last X-Amz-Signature trailer
incidents.fix: change to `sed 's/^[^=]*=//' | tr -d '\\'` in both URL_LINE and SHA_LINE extractors
incidents.verification_pre_relaunch: dry-run extraction printed https://.../hexa-...tar.gz?X-Amz-Algorithm=... + 64-char hex SHA correctly
incidents.prevention: any extraction of structured values (URLs, JSON, key=value) under `set -e` must use non-greedy or anchored patterns; never trust greedy regex on user-provided opaque strings

incidents.symptom: chain died before step 1 with no stderr captured; process table showed no chain PID running
incidents.root_cause: tool/h100_stage2_post_launch_chain.bash L165 used `declare -A PID_WAIT` (bash 4.0+ associative array) — macOS /bin/bash is 3.2 and shebang was `#!/bin/bash`, so parser errored silently under `exec ... &>log`
incidents.fix: rewrite PID_WAIT as parallel scalars `local wait_p1 wait_p2 wait_p3 wait_p4` (loss-free since only 4 fixed path IDs)
incidents.commit_pending: will land with incident #2 sed fix in one commit
incidents.prevention: any new bash idiom beyond 3.2 (declare -A, mapfile, readarray, ${var,,}) MUST either change shebang to `#!/usr/bin/env bash` + document bash4 dep, OR use portable POSIX equivalents; this script runs from macOS operator shell

incidents.symptom: adapter_pull pulled 4/4 /final/ dirs successfully but did NOT pull /workspace/trained_pN/h_last_raw.json; pods were killed immediately post-pull → h_last_raw data lost
incidents.root_cause: tool/h100_stage2_adapter_pull.bash step 2 only scp'd `/workspace/trained_pN/final/` (the LoRA adapter). h_last_raw.json lives at `/workspace/trained_pN/h_last_raw.json` (one level up, outside `final/`). The file was used as training-complete signal (step 1 waits for it) but never pulled. Pod kill at step 4 destroyed the file.
incidents.downstream_impact: Stage 4 forward-pass artifact missing → Stage 6 Φ gate cannot run → blocks CP1 closure path via r5
incidents.fix: patched adapter_pull.bash step 2 to ALSO scp h_last_raw.json → state/h_last_raw_pN_TRAINED_pending.json (non-fatal warn if missing). Will land with incident #2/#3 fix.
recovery_options: A. Launch forward-pass-only mini pod (1 H100, sequential across 4 bases, ~40min, ~$3-5) — regenerate 4 h_last_raw files from the pulled adapters
B. Local Mac CPU/MPS forward-pass (free, ~2-4h wall, ~OOM risk for 12B Gemma)
C. Accept r5 as training-only; skip Φ gate; use r4 edge-FAIL as provisional result — breaks full CP1 decision
incidents.prevention: adapter_pull step 2 now pulls both; convergence mistake #5 (2026-04-24 r3) was inverse error (pulled only h_last_raw, lost adapter) — now pull BOTH, keep BOTH as independent artifacts

code_fixes_committed.change: declare -A PID_WAIT → plain wait_p1..p4 scalars (bash 3.2 compat)
sed 's/.*=//' → sed 's/^[^=]*=//' | tr -d '\\' (non-greedy, strip escape backslashes)

artifacts_missing_or_pending: state/atlas_convergence_witness.jsonl r5 entry — Stage 7, pending operator decision on next round

artifacts_landed_recovery_A.desc: full cross-substrate result (spectra + null bootstrap + pair table)

recovery_A_forward_pass.new_failure_pair_in_r5: p2_p4 (L2 0.2230, KL 0.1520) — didn't fail in r4
recovery_A_forward_pass.method_parity_verified: Same EVAL 16 prompts + hidden_dim_truncated=256 + schema anima/h_last_raw/1 as r2/r3/r4 (bit-exact driver logic copied from tool/h100_stage2_post_launch_chain.bash lines 262-270)

next_round_hypothesis: r14 corpus expansion (1200 vs r13 subset) did NOT close the p3_p4 edge — actually widened it (+0.033 L2). Hypothesis: Korean-heavy content pushes Gemma-3-12B (p4) and Mistral-Nemo-12B (p3) further apart in spectrum space because their tokenizer vocabularies differ most there (262K vs 32K).
Newly-failing p2_p4 (Llama-Gemma) mirrors the same tokenizer-divergence pattern for larger-param Gemma path.

Next round options: (a) increase max_steps 300→600 to push towards spectrum convergence (costs ~2x GPU-hr per pod), (b) reduce substrate diversity by swapping p4 back to gemma-3-12b fine-tune of same corpus on an Apache substrate, (c) accept FAIL as evidence that the 4-path hypothesis requires tighter tokenizer normalization (pre-registered #17 exit criterion — this IS the finding).

training_timeline.all_h_last_raw_present_on_pods: 2026-04-24T15:20:11Z (~16 min training wall, much faster than r4 27min due to fully pre-cached bases + warmer disk)

next_step_options: Recovery A (recommended): 1-H100 forward-pass pod sequentially loads each base + LoRA, writes h_last_raw_pN.json, pulls, kills. Cost ~$3-5, wall ~40min. Produces Stage 4 artifacts.
Then Stage 5 CP1 gen (10 prompts) via cp1_serve_launch.bash on p1 adapter + evaluate
Then Stage 6 Φ gate: hexa run tool/phi_4path_gate.hexa --tag r5
Stage 7: append state/atlas_convergence_witness.jsonl with r5 verdict + failures
Stage 8: h_last_raw_rotate + adapter R2 archive + trained_adapters → trained_adapters_r5 backup
invariants_honored: HEXA-FIRST: no .py files authored in repo (pod-side training drivers only, .gitignore enforces)
auto-charge: no balance/budget halting during H100 chains
convergence_store: this file lives under state/convergence/ not flat state/
max-parallelism: no GPU count cap; budget metric = gpu_hours
no_idle_pods: never pause for A/B/C/D menus while pods are live

diagnostic_before_retry_x3: H-DIAG3 rule — 2 consecutive same-strategy fails require new diagnostic evidence before attempt 3 (honored: incident #2 dry-run verified URL extraction before attempt 3 relaunch)
loss-free: all bug fixes this session are root-cause fixes, not workarounds (bash3.2 compat, sed non-greedy, adapter_pull step-2 augment)
prevention_index_additions: bash_version_compat: macOS /bin/bash is 3.2; any declare -A / mapfile / ${var,,} in tool/*.bash requires `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang + documented bash4 dep OR POSIX rewrite
greedy_regex_on_opaque_strings: extraction of values from vendor-provided URLs/JSON must use anchored or non-greedy patterns (`s/^[^=]*=//`, not `s/.*=//`)
session_detachment: chain launches from Bash tool must survive parent shell termination — use setsid/screen/systemd-run, never rely on nohup alone under interactive parent
both_artifacts_pulled: adapter_pull must scp BOTH `final/` (LoRA) AND `h_last_raw.json` (forward-pass data) before pod kill; losing either costs a forward-pass rerun
meta_lessons: Training itself was fast (16 min) — the r2/r3/r4 '27min/path' estimate was stale (more corpus, but better cache). Re-baseline GPU-hour budgets downward for r6+.

Launch overhead (bootstrap + SSH + driver ship) is ~5 min per attempt on stable chain. Failed attempts cost $3-5 each — H-DIAG3 dry-run-before-retry is high ROI (attempt-3 succeeded because incident #2 fix was verified locally before relaunch).
Post-training scp is 17 min, dominated by p4 Gemma-3-12b 2.1G adapter (LoRA rank 128 bf16). If tighter CP1 iteration needed, smaller adapter ranks would significantly speed scp.

Convergence mistake #5 (2026-04-24 r3, lost adapter) and incident #4 this session (lost h_last_raw) are DUAL failures of the same root: adapter_pull was iteratively patched to pull one artifact then the other, never both. Now fixed to pull both with independent failure paths.
